header: Pass your accessibility review before it starts
content:
Every element in the library starts from the same baseline: semantic landmarks, a strict heading order, and visible focus on every interactive control. Alt-text fields are part of each image schema, so editors are prompted for descriptions instead of retrofitting them under deadline pressure.

An 11-category template audit runs in CI with zero tolerance — no inline styles, no hardcoded colors — and the WCAG-minded markup is verified across all 255 elements on every change, with WCAG 2.2-checked contrast on all 15 presets. Your project starts from an accessible baseline instead of paying for one shortly before launch.
